event: Abstract Assemblage Sculpture Evoking a Lighthouse Form
symbols: the vertical stacked form — a totem-like presence built from humble found materials
the lighthouse shape — a navigational beacon, guiding light on the road
colored geometric squares — small accents of vivid color punctuating the weathered wood surfaces
description: A vertical assemblage sculpture made from weathered wood pieces and painted geometric elements, photographed against a plain white background. The form loosely suggests a lighthouse or roadside marker: a semicircular top piece sits atop a narrow vertical plank, with a triangular middle element, and a wider base with projecting horizontal arms. Small painted squares in blue, orange, teal, and gold are distributed across the wood surfaces. The materials are rough, worn, and show the patina of age. The overall effect is a playful, abstract totem with folk art sensibility.

_meta.title: St. Martin's Eye of the Road
